name: gmail-automation
description: Automate Gmail tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): send/reply, search, labels, drafts, attachments. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Send new emails and reply to existing threads
Search emails using Gmail query syntax (sender, date, labels, attachments)
Create, rename, and delete custom labels
Apply or remove labels on messages in bulk (up to 1000)
Create, edit, and send email drafts with attachments
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation (SKILL.md - 276 lines of markdown). All 124 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES: backtick patterns are markdown code formatting, not shell execution. The hardcoded URL is the legitimate Rube MCP endpoint. No executable code exists in this repository. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Stop manually managing your inbox. This skill enables AI assistants to send emails, search messages, manage labels, and handle drafts through Gmail's official API via Rube MCP.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active Gmail OAuth authorization
Cannot send emails larger than 25MB (Gmail API limit)
Custom label operations require label IDs, not display names
Cannot modify immutable system labels (SENT, DRAFT, CHAT)

useCases.title: Executive Email Assistant
useCases.description: Automate routine email responses, search for specific messages by sender or date, and organize inbox with custom labels for priority management.

useCases.title: Customer Support Automation
useCases.description: Search for customer inquiries by subject or sender, reply to support tickets, and apply labels to track issue status and categorization.

useCases.title: Personal Inbox Manager
useCases.description: Automatically label incoming emails, archive processed messages, create drafts for follow-ups, and search for important emails with attachments.

promptTemplates.title: Send a Simple Email
promptTemplates.prompt: Send an email to john@example.com with subject 'Meeting Tomorrow' and body 'Hi John, are you available for a meeting at 2 PM tomorrow? Let me know. Thanks!'

promptTemplates.title: Search and Reply
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all unread emails from sarah@company.com from the last week. Show me the most recent one and help me draft a reply.

promptTemplates.title: Label Management
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new label called 'Q1-Projects' with a blue background. Then find all emails with 'Q1' in the subject from this month and apply this label to them.

promptTemplates.title: Draft with Attachment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a draft email to team@example.com with subject 'Weekly Report' and attach the file at /documents/report.pdf. Do not send yet, let me review first.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before any Gmail operation
Use GMAIL_LIST_LABELS to resolve label names to IDs before applying labels to messages
For bulk operations, chunk message batches to stay under the 1000 message limit per API call
antiPatterns: Do not use web UI message IDs or UUIDs - Gmail API requires 15-16 character hex message IDs
Do not set subject parameter when replying to threads - it creates a new thread instead
Do not use label: prefix for system states - use is:unread, is:starred, is:snoozed instead

faq.question: How do I connect my Gmail account to this skill?
faq.answer: Add the Rube MCP server (https://rube.app/mcp) to your client configuration. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'gmail' and follow the OAuth link to authorize Google access.

faq.question: Can I send emails with attachments?
faq.answer: Yes. First download the file to get an s3key, then include it in the attachment parameter with mimetype (e.g., 'application/pdf') and filename. Total size must be under 25MB.

faq.question: How do I find a specific email?
faq.answer: Use GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS with Gmail query syntax. Examples: 'from:alice subject:invoice', 'is:unread after:2024/01/01', or 'has:attachment label:work'.

faq.question: What is the difference between thread_id and message_id?
faq.answer: thread_id groups related emails in a conversation (used for replies). message_id identifies a single email (used for fetching details or single-message operations).

faq.question: Can I automate sending emails without approval?
faq.answer: GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL sends immediately. For drafts requiring review, use GMAIL_CREATE_EMAIL_DRAFT first, then GMAIL_SEND_DRAFT only after explicit user confirmation.

faq.question: Why can't I delete a label?
faq.answer: System labels (INBOX, SENT, DRAFT, SPAM) cannot be deleted. Also ensure you're using the label ID (e.g., 'Label_123'), not the display name, for delete operations.
